revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
'''天祖若宮八幡宮'''（てんそわかみやはちまんぐう）は[[東京都]][[練馬区]][[関町北]]三丁目にある、[[神社]]である。[[1974年]]（昭和49年）若宮八幡宮と天祖神社を合わせて天祖若宮八幡宮と呼ぶようになった。祭神は天祖神社には[[大日孁貴女尊]]（おおひるめむちのみこと）、[[狭依姫命]]（さよりひめのみこと）、[[倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと）を合祀。若宮八幡宮には[[八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）、[[仁徳天皇]]を奉斉。例大祭は9月28日。
== 由緒 == 若宮八幡宮は、奈良朝時代に武蔵関塞が設けられたとき、関塞守護神として奉斉されたと伝えられている。関塞廃止後、長い歳月を経て[[慶長]]年間に関村開村のおり村民の氏神となった。 天祖神社は『新編武蔵風土記稿』開村の条に「三十番神社、村ノ鎮守ナリ本立寺持」と記されているとおり、もとは「番神さま」と呼ばれていたが、[[明治維新]]の[[神仏分離]]により天祖神社と改称された。
== 交通 == [[西武新宿線]]「[[武蔵関駅]]」 徒歩5分 [[JR]][[中央線]]「[[荻窪駅]]」よりバス20分、「[[三鷹駅]]」よりバス10分「関町北2丁目」下車 徒歩1分
{{DEFAULTSORT:てんそわかみやはちまんぐう}} [[Category:練馬区の神社]]

revision.comment: /* 交通 */ リンク先を変更しました。

'''天祖若宮八幡宮'''（てんそわかみやはちまんぐう）は[[東京都]][[練馬区]][[関町北]]三丁目にある、[[神社]]である。[[1974年]]（昭和49年）若宮八幡宮と天祖神社を合わせて天祖若宮八幡宮と呼ぶようになった。祭神は天祖神社には[[大日孁貴女尊]]（おおひるめむちのみこと）、[[狭依姫命]]（さよりひめのみこと）、[[倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと）を合祀。若宮八幡宮には[[八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）、[[仁徳天皇]]を奉斉。例大祭は9月28日。
== 由緒 == 若宮八幡宮は、奈良朝時代に武蔵関塞が設けられたとき、関塞守護神として奉斉されたと伝えられている。関塞廃止後、長い歳月を経て[[慶長]]年間に関村開村のおり村民の氏神となった。 天祖神社は『新編武蔵風土記稿』開村の条に「三十番神社、村ノ鎮守ナリ本立寺持」と記されているとおり、もとは「番神さま」と呼ばれていたが、[[明治維新]]の[[神仏分離]]により天祖神社と改称された。
== 交通 == *[[西武鉄道|西武]][[西武新宿線|新宿線]]「[[武蔵関駅]]」 徒歩5分 *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線|中央線]]「[[荻窪駅]]」よりバス20分、「[[三鷹駅]]」よりバス10分「関町北2丁目」下車 徒歩1分
{{DEFAULTSORT:てんそわかみやはちまんぐう}} [[Category:練馬区の神社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2011年1月}} '''天祖若宮八幡宮'''（てんそわかみやはちまんぐう）は[[東京都]][[練馬区]][[関町北]]三丁目にある、[[神社]]である。[[1974年]]（昭和49年）若宮八幡宮と天祖神社を合わせて天祖若宮八幡宮と呼ぶようになった。祭神は天祖神社には[[大日孁貴女尊]]（おおひるめむちのみこと）、[[狭依姫命]]（さよりひめのみこと）、[[倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと）を合祀。若宮八幡宮には[[八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）、[[仁徳天皇]]を奉斉。例大祭は9月28日。
== 由緒 == 若宮八幡宮は、奈良朝時代に武蔵関塞が設けられたとき、関塞守護神として奉斉されたと伝えられている。関塞廃止後、長い歳月を経て[[慶長]]年間に関村開村のおり村民の氏神となった。 天祖神社は『新編武蔵風土記稿』開村の条に「三十番神社、村ノ鎮守ナリ本立寺持」と記されているとおり、もとは「番神さま」と呼ばれていたが、[[明治維新]]の[[神仏分離]]により天祖神社と改称された。
== 交通 == *[[西武鉄道|西武]][[西武新宿線|新宿線]]「[[武蔵関駅]]」 徒歩5分 *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線|中央線]]「[[荻窪駅]]」よりバス20分、「[[三鷹駅]]」よりバス10分「関町北2丁目」下車 徒歩1分

revision.comment: 大日霊貴神社の履歴参照、[[Special:Contributions/Dewiki|Dewiki]] ([[User talk:Dewiki|会話]]) による ID:67559754 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]]
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2011年1月}} [[ファイル:Tenso Wakamiya hachimangu main worship building - aug 8 2015.jpg|thumb|拝殿]] '''天祖若宮八幡宮'''（てんそわかみやはちまんぐう）は[[東京都]][[練馬区]][[関町北]]三丁目にある、[[神社]]である。[[1974年]]（昭和49年）若宮八幡宮と天祖神社を合わせて天祖若宮八幡宮と呼ぶようになった。祭神は天祖神社には[[大日孁貴女尊]]（おおひるめむちのみこと）、[[狭依姫命]]（さよりひめのみこと）、[[倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと）を合祀。若宮八幡宮には[[八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）、[[仁徳天皇]]を奉斉。例大祭は9月28日。
== 由緒 == 若宮八幡宮は、[[奈良時代]]に武蔵関塞が設けられたとき、関塞守護神として奉斉されたと伝えられている。関塞廃止後、長い歳月を経て[[慶長]]年間に関村（現在の[[関町北]]、[[関町南]]、[[関町東]]、[[立野町]]に相当）開村のおり村民の[[氏神]]となった。 天祖神社は『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』開村の条に「三十番神社、村ノ鎮守ナリ本立寺持」と記されているとおり、もとは「番神さま」と呼ばれていたが、[[明治維新]]の[[神仏分離]]により天祖神社と改称された。
== 交通 == *[[西武鉄道|西武]][[西武新宿線|新宿線]]「[[武蔵関駅]]」 徒歩5分 *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線|中央線]]「[[荻窪駅]]」よりバス20分、「[[三鷹駅]]」よりバス10分「関町北2丁目」下車 徒歩1分
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんそわかみやはちまんくう}} [[Category:東京都区部の神社]] [[Category:練馬区の歴史]] [[Category:練馬区の建築物]]

{{出典の明記|date=2011年1月}} [[ファイル:Tenso Wakamiya hachimangu main worship building - aug 8 2015.jpg|thumb|拝殿]] '''天祖若宮八幡宮'''（てんそわかみやはちまんぐう）は[[東京都]][[練馬区]][[関町北]]三丁目にある、[[神社]]である。[[1974年]]（昭和49年）若宮八幡宮と天祖神社を合わせて天祖若宮八幡宮と呼ぶようになった<ref>{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=練馬の神社 |publisher=練馬区教育委員会 |year=2006-3-1 |page=59 |isbn=}}</ref>。祭神は天祖神社には[[大日孁貴女尊]]（おおひるめむちのみこと）、[[狭依姫命]]（さよりひめのみこと）、[[倉稲魂命]]（うがのみたまのみこと）を合祀。若宮八幡宮には[[八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）、[[仁徳天皇]]を奉斉。例大祭は9月28日。
== 由緒 == 若宮八幡宮は、[[奈良時代]]に武蔵関塞が設けられたとき、関塞守護神として奉斉されたと伝えられている。関塞廃止後、長い歳月を経て[[慶長]]年間に関村（現在の[[関町北]]、[[関町南]]、[[関町東]]、[[立野町]]に相当）開村のおり村民の[[氏神]]となった。 天祖神社は『[[新編武蔵風土記稿]]』開村の条に「三十番神社、村ノ鎮守ナリ本立寺持」と記されているとおり、もとは「番神さま」と呼ばれていたが、[[明治維新]]の[[神仏分離]]により天祖神社と改称された。
== 交通 == *[[西武鉄道|西武]][[西武新宿線|新宿線]]「[[武蔵関駅]]」 徒歩5分 *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線|中央線]]「[[荻窪駅]]」よりバス20分、「[[三鷹駅]]」よりバス10分「関町北2丁目」下車 徒歩1分 == 出典 == {{reflist}}
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:てんそわかみやはちまんくう}} [[Category:東京都区部の神社]] [[Category:練馬区の歴史]] [[Category:練馬区の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－１.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, Sekimachikita 3-chome | Deities={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Festival=September 28 }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[1974]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神}}, identified with {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}} (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashiseki Station|ja|武蔵関駅}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|ja|西武新宿線}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|ja|荻窪駅}} on the {{ill|JR Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|ja|三鷹駅}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Emperor Nintoku]] [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Festival=September 28 }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[1974]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神}}, identified with {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}} (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashiseki Station|ja|武蔵関駅}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|ja|西武新宿線}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|ja|荻窪駅}} on the {{ill|JR Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|ja|三鷹駅}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Emperor Nintoku]] [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]]

{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Festival=September 28 }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[1974]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神}}, identified with {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}} (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashiseki Station|ja|武蔵関駅}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|ja|西武新宿線}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|ja|荻窪駅}} on the {{ill|JR Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|ja|三鷹駅}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Emperor Nintoku]] [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qq]]

Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashiseki Station|ja|武蔵関駅}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|ja|西武新宿線}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|ja|荻窪駅}} on the {{ill|JR Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|ja|三鷹駅}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Emperor Nintoku]] [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashiseki Station|ja|武蔵関駅}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|ja|西武新宿線}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|ja|荻窪駅}} on the {{ill|JR Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|ja|三鷹駅}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Emperor Nintoku]] [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashiseki Station|ja|武蔵関駅}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|ja|西武新宿線}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|ja|荻窪駅}} on the {{ill|JR Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|ja|三鷹駅}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Emperor Nintoku]] [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:enso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Festival=September 28 }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[1974]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神}}, identified with {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}} (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashiseki Station|ja|武蔵関駅}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|ja|西武新宿線}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|ja|荻窪駅}} on the {{ill|JR Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|ja|三鷹駅}}, 10 minutes by bus. {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Festival=September 28 }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[1974]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神}}, identified with {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}} (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} with the opening of Sekimura. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|ja|三鷹駅}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Emperor Nintoku]] [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:enso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－１.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, Sekimachikita 3-chome | deity={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Festival=September 28 }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[1974]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神}}, identified with {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}} (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashiseki Station|ja|武蔵関駅}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|ja|西武新宿線}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|ja|荻窪駅}} on the {{ill|JR Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|ja|三鷹駅}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Emperor Nintoku]] [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:enso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{Infobox shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－１.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location={{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, Sekimachikita 3-chome | deity={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | Festival=September 28 }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, {{ill|Nerima Ward|ja|練馬区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}, Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[1974]] ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神}}, identified with {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}} as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}} (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the {{ill|Keichō era|ja|慶長}} with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|ja|神仏分離}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}}, which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashiseki Station|ja|武蔵関駅}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|ja|西武新宿線}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|ja|荻窪駅}} on the {{ill|JR Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|ja|三鷹駅}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Musashiseki Park|ja|武蔵関公園}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Emperor Nintoku]] [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:enso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] | Festival=September 28 | prefecture={{ill|Sekimachi-Kita|qid=Q11656138}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.7264303|N|139.570587|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]] (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|qq=|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from [[:en:Musashi-Seki Station|Musashiseki Station]] on the [[:en:Seibu Shinjuku Line|Seibu Shinjuku Line]].{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From [[:en:Ogikubo Station|Ogikubo Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Chūō Main Line]], 20 minutes by bus. From [[:en:Mitaka Station|Mitaka Station]], 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}}

The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]] (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|qq=|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|qq=|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from [[:en:Musashi-Seki Station|Musashiseki Station]] on the [[:en:Seibu Shinjuku Line|Seibu Shinjuku Line]].{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From [[:en:Ogikubo Station|Ogikubo Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Chūō Main Line]], 20 minutes by bus. From [[:en:Mitaka Station|Mitaka Station]], 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}}

Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from [[:en:Musashi-Seki Station|Musashiseki Station]] on the [[:en:Seibu Shinjuku Line|Seibu Shinjuku Line]].{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From [[:en:Ogikubo Station|Ogikubo Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Chūō Main Line]], 20 minutes by bus. From [[:en:Mitaka Station|Mitaka Station]], 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17218549]] Wikidata name: Tenso Wakamiya Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Sekimachikita 3-chome | deity={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] | Festival=September 28 | prefecture={{ill|Sekimachi-Kita|WD=Q11656138}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.7264303|N|139.570587|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]] (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from [[:en:Musashi-Seki Station|Musashiseki Station]] on the [[:en:Seibu Shinjuku Line|Seibu Shinjuku Line]].{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From [[:en:Ogikubo Station|Ogikubo Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Chūō Main Line]], 20 minutes by bus. From [[:en:Mitaka Station|Mitaka Station]], 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17218549]] Wikidata name: Tenso Wakamiya Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Sekimachikita 3-chome | deity={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] | Festival=September 28 | prefecture={{ill|Sekimachi-Kita|WD=Q11656138}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.7264303|N|139.570587|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]] (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from [[:en:Musashi-Seki Station|Musashiseki Station]] on the [[:en:Seibu Shinjuku Line|Seibu Shinjuku Line]].{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From [[:en:Ogikubo Station|Ogikubo Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Chūō Main Line]], 20 minutes by bus. From [[:en:Mitaka Station|Mitaka Station]], 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17218549]] Wikidata name: Tenso Wakamiya Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Sekimachikita 3-chome | deity={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] | Festival=September 28 | prefecture={{ill|Sekimachi-Kita|WD=Q11656138}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.7264303|N|139.570587|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]] (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from [[:en:Musashi-Seki Station|Musashiseki Station]] on the [[:en:Seibu Shinjuku Line|Seibu Shinjuku Line]].{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From [[:en:Ogikubo Station|Ogikubo Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Chūō Main Line]], 20 minutes by bus. From [[:en:Mitaka Station|Mitaka Station]], 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17218549]] Wikidata name: Tenso Wakamiya Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Sekimachikita 3-chome | deity={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] | Festival=September 28 | prefecture={{ill|Sekimachi-Kita|WD=Q11656138}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.7264303|N|139.570587|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]] (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from [[:en:Musashi-Seki Station|Musashiseki Station]] on the [[:en:Seibu Shinjuku Line|Seibu Shinjuku Line]].{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From [[:en:Ogikubo Station|Ogikubo Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Chūō Main Line]], 20 minutes by bus. From [[:en:Mitaka Station|Mitaka Station]], 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}

Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]].
