Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17218549}}

[[commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17218549]] Wikidata name: Tenso Wakamiya Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Sekimachikita 3-chome | deity={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] | Festival=September 28 | prefecture={{ill|Sekimachi-Kita|WD=Q11656138}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.7264303|N|139.570587|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]] (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from [[:en:Musashi-Seki Station|Musashiseki Station]] on the [[:en:Seibu Shinjuku Line|Seibu Shinjuku Line]].{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From [[:en:Ogikubo Station|Ogikubo Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Chūō Main Line]], 20 minutes by bus. From [[:en:Mitaka Station|Mitaka Station]], 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17218549}}

Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17218549]] Wikidata name: Tenso Wakamiya Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Sekimachikita 3-chome | deity={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] | Festival=September 28 | prefecture={{ill|Sekimachi-Kita|WD=Q11656138}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.7264303|N|139.570587|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]] (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from [[:en:Musashi-Seki Station|Musashiseki Station]] on the [[:en:Seibu Shinjuku Line|Seibu Shinjuku Line]].{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From [[:en:Ogikubo Station|Ogikubo Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Chūō Main Line]], 20 minutes by bus. From [[:en:Mitaka Station|Mitaka Station]], 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

From [[:en:Mitaka Station|Mitaka Station]], 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17218549]] Wikidata name: Tenso Wakamiya Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], Sekimachikita 3-chome | deity={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] | Festival=September 28 | prefecture={{ill|Sekimachi-Kita|WD=Q11656138}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.7264303|N|139.570587|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, [[:en:Nerima|Nerima Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]], Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In [[:en:1974|1974]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the [[:en:Ujigami|ujigami]] (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō era]] with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from [[:en:Musashi-Seki Station|Musashiseki Station]] on the [[:en:Seibu Shinjuku Line|Seibu Shinjuku Line]].{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From [[:en:Ogikubo Station|Ogikubo Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|JR Chūō Main Line]], 20 minutes by bus. From [[:en:Mitaka Station|Mitaka Station]], 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

From [[:en:Mitaka Station|Mitaka Station]], 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17218549]] Wikidata name: Tenso Wakamiya Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location={{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward}}, Sekimachikita 3-chome | deity={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} | Festival=September 28 | prefecture={{ill|Sekimachi-Kita|WD=Q11656138}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.7264303|N|139.570587|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=1974}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=ujigami}} (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō era}} with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashi-Seki Station|en|Musashi-Seki Station|WD=Q843304|lt=Musashiseki Station}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|en|Seibu Shinjuku Line|WD=Q195685|lt=Seibu Shinjuku Line}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|en|Ogikubo Station|WD=Q801294|lt=Ogikubo Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=JR Chūō Main Line}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|en|Mitaka Station|WD=Q546655|lt=Mitaka Station}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=ujigami}} (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō era}} with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashi-Seki Station|en|Musashi-Seki Station|WD=Q843304|lt=Musashiseki Station}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|en|Seibu Shinjuku Line|WD=Q195685|lt=Seibu Shinjuku Line}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|en|Ogikubo Station|WD=Q801294|lt=Ogikubo Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=JR Chūō Main Line}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|en|Mitaka Station|WD=Q546655|lt=Mitaka Station}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location={{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward}}, Sekimachikita 3-chome | deity={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} | Festival=September 28 | prefecture={{ill|Sekimachi-Kita|WD=Q11656138}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.7264303|N|139.570587|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis}}, Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=1974}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=ujigami}} (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō era}} with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Hondatsugi Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashi-Seki Station|en|Musashi-Seki Station|WD=Q843304|lt=Musashiseki Station}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|en|Seibu Shinjuku Line|WD=Q195685|lt=Seibu Shinjuku Line}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|en|Ogikubo Station|WD=Q801294|lt=Ogikubo Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=JR Chūō Main Line}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|en|Mitaka Station|WD=Q546655|lt=Mitaka Station}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} | Festival=September 28 | prefecture={{ill|Sekimachi-Kita|WD=Q11656138|1=Sekimachi-Kita}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.7264303|N|139.570587|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward|1=Nerima}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=1974|1=1974}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō era|1=Keichō}} with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Q11520735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashi-Seki Station|en|Musashi-Seki Station|WD=Q843304|lt=Musashiseki Station|1=Musashi-Seki Station}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|en|Seibu Shinjuku Line|WD=Q195685|lt=Seibu Shinjuku Line|1=Seibu Shinjuku Line}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|en|Ogikubo Station|WD=Q801294|lt=Ogikubo Station|1=Ogikubo Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=JR Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|en|Mitaka Station|WD=Q546655|lt=Mitaka Station|1=Mitaka Station}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū]][[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg | name=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | location={{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward|1=Nerima}}, Sekimachikita 3-chome | deity={{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}
{{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}}
{{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} | Festival=September 28 | prefecture={{ill|Sekimachi-Kita|WD=Q11656138|1=Sekimachi-Kita}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.7264303|N|139.570587|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (天祖若宮八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Sekimachikita 3-chome, {{ill|Nerima|en|Nerima|WD=Q232655|lt=Nerima Ward|1=Nerima}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo Metropolis|1=Tokyo}}, Japan. The shrine is situated atop a hill in [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], maintained by Nerima Ward. == Overview == In {{ill|1974|en|1974|WD=Q2478|lt=1974|1=1974}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 49), Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine and Tenso Shrine were merged and began to be collectively referred to as Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine.{{sfn|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} The enshrined deities include {{ill|Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja|大日孁貴女尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神|1=Amaterasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q455602}}, {{ill|Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja|狭依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sayori Hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja|倉稲魂命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} at Tenso Shrine. Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine venerates {{ill|Hachiman Ōkami|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神|1=Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q261637}}, identified with {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. The main festival is held annually on September 28.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == History == [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－２.jpg|thumb|Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine]] [[File:天祖若宮八幡宮－３.jpg|thumb|Shrine grounds]] Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine is said to have been established during the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} as the guardian deity of the Musashi Seki Barrier.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} After the barrier was dismantled, it served as the {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=ujigami|1=Ujigami}} (guardian deity) of Sekimura, which encompasses present-day Sekimachikita, Sekimachi Minami, Sekimachi Higashi, and Tatemachi. The shrine's role was formalized during the {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|WD=Q1328185|lt=Keichō era|1=Keichō}} with the opening of Sekimura. Tenso Shrine was originally referred to as "Banjin-sama" (番神さま), as described in the "Sekimura" section of the {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudokikō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11503943}}: "The Banshin Shrine is the village's guardian deity and belongs to {{ill|Hondatsugi Temple|ja|本立寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondatsugi Temple|1=Q11520735|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11520735}}."{{cite book|和書|title=新編武蔵風土記稿|volume=Volume 13, Toshima District Part 5|chapter=Sekimura|id={{NDLJP|763977/46}}|publisher=Geographical Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6}} During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, it was renamed Tenso Shrine due to the policy of {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}. == Transportation == The shrine is surrounded by dense greenery and forms part of the verdant landscape of Musashino, along with [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]], which features a boating pond. * 5 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashi-Seki Station|en|Musashi-Seki Station|WD=Q843304|lt=Musashiseki Station|1=Musashi-Seki Station}} on the {{ill|Seibu Shinjuku Line|en|Seibu Shinjuku Line|WD=Q195685|lt=Seibu Shinjuku Line|1=Seibu Shinjuku Line}}.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} * From {{ill|Ogikubo Station|en|Ogikubo Station|WD=Q801294|lt=Ogikubo Station|1=Ogikubo Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=JR Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}}, 20 minutes by bus. From {{ill|Mitaka Station|en|Mitaka Station|WD=Q546655|lt=Mitaka Station|1=Mitaka Station}}, 10 minutes by bus. Alight at "Sekimachikita 2-chome," then 1 minute's walk.{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Agency}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|title=Shrines of Nerima|publisher=Nerima Ward Board of Education|year=2006-3-1|page=59|ref={{sfnref|Nerima Ward Board of Education}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nerima/6074/|title=Tenso Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|website=Tokyo Shrine Agency|accessdate=2019-10-21|ref={{sfnref|Tokyo Shrine Agency}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Musashiseki Park|Musashiseki Park]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|天祖若宮八幡宮|version=97985971|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:天祖若宮八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A9%E7%A5%96%E8%8B%A5%E5%AE%AE%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE]] {{Commons category|Tenso-Wakamiya-hachimangū}} {{wikidata link|Q17218549}} [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nerima, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
